intro: Drimia zambesiaca, commonly known as the leopard lily or devil's claw, is a plant species native to southern Africa, characterized by its unique claw-like fruit. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including pain, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Drimia zambesiaca include harpagoside, a glycoside compound responsible for its medicinal effects, as well as other iridoid glycosides. The most common herbal preparations made with Drimia zambesiaca are capsules, tablets, and teas, often used to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in conditions such as arthritis and fibromyalgia.
